Ops for masked arrays.
annotationsN)
missingz
bool | np.ndarrayz
np.ndarray | None)
left
right
	left_mask
right_maskc
    Boolean ``or`` using Kleene logic.
    Values are NA where we have ``NA | NA`` or ``NA | False``.
    ``NA | True`` is considered True.
    Parameters
    ----------
    left, right : ndarray, NA, or bool
        The values of the array.
    left_mask, right_mask : ndarray, optional
        The masks. Only one of these may be None, which implies that
        the associated `left` or `right` value is a scalar.
    Returns
    -------
    result, mask: ndarray[bool]
        The result of the logical or, and the new mask.
    N
methodT)
	kleene_or
raise_for_nan
libmissing
copy
zeros_like)
result
left_false
right_false
mask
>/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/pandas/core/ops/mask_ops.pyr
    Boolean ``xor`` using Kleene logic.
    This is the same as ``or``, with the following adjustments
    * True, True -> False
    * True, NA   -> NA
    Parameters
    ----------
    left, right : ndarray, NA, or bool
        The values of the array.
    left_mask, right_mask : ndarray, optional
        The masks. Only one of these may be None, which implies that
        the associated `left` or `right` value is a scalar.
    Returns
    -------
    result, mask: ndarray[bool]
        The result of the logical xor, and the new mask.
    N
xorr
kleene_xorr
Z	ones_liker
z%bool | libmissing.NAType | np.ndarrayc
    Boolean ``and`` using Kleene logic.
    Values are ``NA`` for ``NA & NA`` or ``True & NA``.
    Parameters
    ----------
    left, right : ndarray, NA, or bool
        The values of the array.
    left_mask, right_mask : ndarray, optional
        The masks. Only one of these may be None, which implies that
        the associated `left` or `right` value is a scalar.
    Returns
    -------
    result, mask: ndarray[bool]
        The result of the logical xor, and the new mask.
    N
andr
kleene_andr
strr
Cannot perform logical 'z
' with floating NaN)
is_floatr
isnan
ValueError)
valuer
__doc__Z
__future__r
numpyr
pandas._libsr
<module>
